sentiment: What is fast is often just what was designed with patience — the speed we feel is the shadow of invisible care.

stories.title: DuckDB Internals: Why Is DuckDB Fast? (Part 1)
stories.summary: A deep dive into the architectural choices — vectorized execution, columnar storage, and careful memory management — that make DuckDB unusually fast for analytical queries.

stories.title: Project Valhalla, Explained: How a Decade of Work Arrives in JDK 28
stories.summary: After ten years of development, Java's Project Valhalla delivers value types in JDK 28, collapsing the performance gap between primitives and objects.

stories.title: So You Want to Define a Well-Known URI
stories.summary: A practical guide to the RFC process for registering new .well-known URIs — the hidden infrastructure that lets disparate systems discover each other.
